steps.title: Use the special triangle ratio
steps.content: An isosceles right triangle is a $45^\circ$-$45^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle. If each leg has length $a$, then the hypotenuse has length $a\sqrt{2}$.

steps.title: Find each leg
steps.content:
The hypotenuse is $62$, so solve for $a$.
$$a\sqrt{2}=62$$
$$a=\frac{62}{\sqrt{2}}=31\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Add the side lengths
steps.content:
The perimeter is the sum of the two legs and the hypotenuse.
$$P=31\sqrt{2}+31\sqrt{2}+62=62+62\sqrt{2}$$
The answer is C.
